title: Eurya wallichiana
taxonomy: Eurya wallichiana is classified within the family Pentaphylacaceae. This family is part of the order Ericales and the clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The species Eurya wallichiana is further categorized within the genus Eurya. The genus Eurya is a group of flowering plants native to Asia and the Indian subcontinent. The classification of Eurya wallichiana is a key aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Eurya wallichiana is utilized in traditional medicine in several ways. The plant's extracts and leaves are reported to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal applications. In some regions, Eurya wallichiana is used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The plant's potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects are thought to be responsible for its use in wound healing and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Eurya wallichiana.

preparations: Eurya wallichiana is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region.
